title: ActivityTemplateFile
description: One file per intent — a JSON object whose `templates` array holds individual offline activity templates for that intent. Validated by toybox.activities.generator.load_templates at startup.

id.description: Stable identifier for this template — used as Activity.template_id and as part of the Phase D step 19 signature.

buckets.description: Time-of-day buckets where this template is eligible. Empty array (or omitted) means 'no time-of-day preference' — equivalent to 'always'. The 'wind_down' bucket is only eligible at hours 19..21.

required_roles.description: Phase K K3: roles a template cannot function without. The eligibility filter rejects templates whose required_roles count exceeds the available toy pool. Each {role_name} placeholder in step text MUST be in required_roles ∪ optional_roles (validated by toybox.activities._validator).

optional_roles.description: Phase K K3: roles the template uses opportunistically. Unfilled slots fall back to generic descriptors at slot-fill time.

recommended_themes.description: Phase K K3: theme tags. Phase L Step L5 removed the K14 embedded-interjection picker that consumed these; the field is accepted for backward compatibility with the existing template catalog and remains available for future surfaces.

role.description: Phase K K1 role taxonomy. Source of truth: toybox.activities.roles.Role. Enum values mirrored here so the jsonschema layer can reject typos before they reach the Pydantic layer.

theme.description: Phase K K1 theme taxonomy + Phase M M8 ``feelings`` SEL addition. Source of truth: toybox.activities.themes.Theme. Enum values mirrored here so the jsonschema layer can reject typos before they reach the Pydantic layer.

text.description: Step text. Supports {slot} and {toy} placeholders plus the parametric slots filled by toybox.activities.slots.SlotRegistry: {room}, {action_verb}, {adjective}, {prop}, {body_part}, {count}. Phase K K3 also permits {role_name} placeholders (one per role declared in the template's required_roles ∪ optional_roles arrays). Unknown {placeholder} names are echoed back verbatim at substitution time so the typo surfaces visibly. The lint script (planned at tools/lint_templates) will enforce the whitelist.

kind.description: Phase K K3: step kind. Defaults to 'text' for legacy steps that omit the field. 'fork' is the K3 explicit spelling of the existing choices-bearing shape; 'song' and 'joke' deliver interjections (validator gates that they reference a corpus entry via corpus_id OR carry auto=true).

corpus_id.description: Phase K K3: corpus entry id for 'song' / 'joke' kinds. Mutually exclusive with auto=true. Corpus loading lives in K10 (jokes) and K11 (songs); K3 only gates the SHAPE.

auto.description: Phase K K3: when true on a 'song' / 'joke' step, the engine picks a corpus entry at activity-creation time using the template's recommended_themes. Mutually exclusive with corpus_id.

action_slot.description: Phase F Step F6 action vocabulary key. One of the 10 fixed slots in toybox.image_gen.models.ACTION_SLOTS, or null to render no sprite. The actual vocabulary enforcement lives in toybox.activities.generator._parse_template so a typo fails LOUDLY (the JSON-schema layer here intentionally leaves the value free-form so the parse layer is the single source of truth — keeping the enum in two places risks drift).

element_id.description: Phase M Step M3 — optional reference to a Periodic Table element corpus entry (``data/elements/elements.json``). Composite id ``<symbol-lower>-<atomic_number>`` (e.g. ``au-79``). When present, the kiosk renders an ElementCard above the step text. Cross-corpus resolution is gated by toybox.activities._validator (unknown ids fail at template load with the id in the message). No persona-side gating per phase-m-plan.md §6.9.

question.description: Phase R Step R3 — optional Q&A gate. When set, the kiosk displays the question text below the step body and replaces Next with 'Waiting for parent…' until the parent approves/skips (or, with Phase W qa_grading on + an expected_answer, the advance handler auto-grades the recent transcript). additionalProperties is false on this object, so the field MUST be declared here for a template to author it — without this entry the JSON-schema validator rejects the whole template and the gate is unreachable for real activities.

expected_answer.description: Phase W Step W3 — optional canonical answer text for Q&A auto-grading. Only meaningful alongside `question`. When BOTH are set AND the household `qa_grading` dial is not 'off', the advance handler auto-grades the last ~30s of transcript against this text and, on a confident match, resolves the R3 gate without a parent tap. NULL opts the step out of auto-grading (falls back to the parent-tap path).

id.description: Phase G: stable identifier for branch targeting; required only on steps referenced as a branch target. Lowercase alphanumerics and underscores; first character alphanumeric. Validator (toybox.activities._validator) enforces uniqueness within a template.

next.description: Phase G: explicit successor step id. Mutually exclusive with `choices` (validated by JSON-schema `not` constraint above plus the load-time graph validator). When neither `next` nor `choices` is set, the runtime falls through to the next step in the array (or terminates at the last step).

choices.description: Phase G: branching choice point. The kiosk renders one button per entry; `next` resolves to the chosen successor's step id. Mutually exclusive with `next`.

label.description: Button text shown to the kid. Supports the same slot placeholders as `text`.

next.description: Step id this branch advances to.
